title: murraya euchrestifolia
intro: Murraya euchrestifolia is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the Chinese box orange or Chinese boxwood. It is a small evergreen tree or shrub native to southern China and widely cultivated in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Murraya euchrestifolia include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures.
